channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump's immigration crackdown leaves some families weighing the risk of sending kids to school
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — As President Donald Trump cracks down on immigrants in the U.S. illegally, some families are wondering if it is safe to send their children to school.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, MORIAH BALINGIT, BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP-led states are emboldened to keep rolling back trans rights. Democrats struggle with a response
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — Republicans and Democrats in Kansas agree that concerns about the economy drove voters to support President Donald Trump by a 16% margin. They also know that ads from Trump and others targeting transgender rights resonated with voters.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, JOHN HANNA and NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump defends pardons for rioters and suggests Proud Boys could have place in politics
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on his first full day in office Tuesday defended his decision to grant clemency to people convicted of assaulting police officers during the 2021 attack on the Capitol and suggested there could be a place in American politics for the Proud Boys and Oath Keepe
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Coast Guard surging assets to Gulf of America, other waterways to support Trump’s executive orders
item.description: In support of President Trump's executive orders, the Coast Guard will send additional resources to maritime borders, the military branch said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration throws out policies limiting migrant arrests at sensitive spots like churches
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Officers enforcing immigration laws will now be able to arrest migrants at sensitive locations like schools and churches after the Trump administration threw out policies limiting where those arrests could happen as the new president seeks to make good on campaign promises to carry
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump defends pardoning of violent Jan. 6 offenders
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday defended signing mass pardons for rioters charged in connection with the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol attack, including those who were violent offenders, a move that has drawn criticism from Democrats and some Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: U.S. throws out policies limiting arrests of migrants at sensitive locations like schools, churches
item.description: Officers enforcing immigration laws will now be able to arrest migrants at sensitive locations like schools and churches after the Trump administration threw out policies limiting where those arrests could happen.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration directs all federal diversity, equity and inclusion staff be put on leave
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration is moving to end affirmative action in federal contracting and is directing that all federal diversity, equity and inclusion staff be put on paid leave and eventually be laid off.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Olson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New Ohio and Florida Senate-appointees sworn in as Vance and Rubio’s replacements
item.description: Two new senators were sworn in on Tuesday to replace new Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California sues Trump administration over executive order ending birthright citizenship
item.description: (FOX40.COM) — California Attorney General Rob Bonta announced his office would be joining 17 other states in suing the federal government over the newly inaugurated President Donald Trump's executive order ending birthright citizenship for those born in the country. The 14th Amendment to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Jacque Porter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At inaugural prayer service, bishop pleads for Trump to ‘have mercy’ on LGBTQ+ people and migrants
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — At the inaugural prayer service, the Right Rev. Mariann Budde, the Episcopal bishop of Washington, made a direct appeal to President Donald Trump to have mercy on the LGBTQ+ community and undocumented migrant workers.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY and GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 4 states sue to block illegal migrants from census count used to assign congressional seats, electoral votes
item.description: Louisiana, Ohio, Kansas and West Virginia sued to block illegal immigrants from being counted when apportioning congressional seats and electoral votes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democratic AGs sue Trump over birthright citizenship executive order
item.description: Twenty-two Democrat-led states and two cities challenged President Trump’s executive order restricting birthright citizenship, that on Tuesday kicked off the first legal battles between his new Justice Department and Democratic attorneys general.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court declines to step into former GOP Rep. Steve King's meme fight
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday declined to revive former Rep. Steve King’s (R-Iowa) bid to receive attorneys’ fees in a copyright fight that stemmed from a meme posted by his campaign.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Cheney responds to Trump: 'Neither lies nor the liar' age well
item.description: Former Rep. Liz Cheney (R-Wyo.) blasted President Trump for false statements he made about the now-disbanded Jan. 6 committee, calling them a “reminder that neither lies nor the liar who tells them get better with age.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Examining Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: DeSantis: North Florida could see several inches of snow
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis warned of potential snowfall this week, amid the freezing temperatures impacting northern Florida.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court won’t take up fight over Pennsylvania mail ballot date requirement
item.description: The Supreme Court said Tuesday it won’t hear civil rights groups’ appeal seeking to invalidate Pennsylvania's dating requirement for mail ballots, which has invalidated thousands of votes each recent election and become a central legal fight over voting in the key swing state.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Why Biden pardoned Mark Milley ahead of Trump taking office
item.description: Former President Joe Biden's pre-emptive pardon for retired Gen. Mark Milley, the former chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, will give the retired military official a shield against any action that President Donald Trump might take against him amid their highly public feud.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's Cabinet picks are set for Senate hearings. Here's the schedule
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nominations process for President Donald Trump's appointees is moving forward this week, with Senate hearings scheduled for several of his Cabinet picks. Many nominees have met with senators individually.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Senate panel advances Noem nomination for Homeland Security secretary
item.description: A Senate committee cleared President Trump's nominee to lead the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) with a bipartisan vote, paving the way for a Senate floor vote. The Senate Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs Committee advanced South Dakota Gov. Kristi Noem (R) by a 13-2 vote.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Court cases could shape
item.description: The Minnesota Supreme Court will hear arguments this week over whether the Republican-only start to session in the House is lawful. Two court decisions late last week have also set the stage for more partisan standoffs.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump pardons nearly all Jan. 6 rioters on first day of presidency
item.description: President Trump pardoned nearly all Jan. 6 defendants on Monday, making good on a long-standing campaign promise just hours into his second presidency.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Immigration top priority for Trump and Florida lawmakers
item.description: Solving the illegal immigration crisis is a priority for both President Donald Trump and Florida Governor Ron DeSantis.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signs executive order to pause TikTok ban, provide immunity to tech firms
item.description: The order follows TikTok going dark for about 14 hours after the Supreme Court upheld a law prohibiting the service from operating in the U.S. unless it breaks away from its parent company in China.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Trump issues pardons for participants in the Jan. 6 riot at the U.S. Capitol
item.description: The pardons fulfilled Trump’s promise to release supporters who tried to help him overturn his election defeat four years ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump completes historic comeback, vowing to turn around nation in decline
item.description: President Trump completed a monumental political comeback on Monday, taking the oath of office for a second time and vowing to turn around a nation he insisted was in decline.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Ratcliffe moves closer to CIA chief confirmation
item.description: John Ratcliffe took a step closer to being confirmed to lead the CIA on Monday as the Senate Intelligence Committee advanced his nomination. The panel advanced his nomination 14 to 3 shortly after President Trump was sworn into office. All three "no" votes were cast by Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Trump seeks to end telework for federal workers
item.description: President Trump has signed an executive action ordering federal agencies to bring their workers back to the office full time. Roughly 1.1 million federal employees are telework-eligible.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Ramaswamy won't serve on Trump's DOGE as he mulls run for Ohio governor
item.description: Ramaswamy, who sought the Republican nomination for president in 2024, has signaled plans to run for governor of Ohio.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Senate panel advances Defense secretary nominee Hegseth on party-line vote
item.description: The Senate Armed Services Committee on Monday voted to advance Pete Hegseth, President Trump’s nominee to serve as secretary of Defense, to the floor. The 14-13 vote was strictly along party lines.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Expert: Trump can’t override Minnesota state law with executive orders
item.description: University of Minnesota political science professor Kathryn Pearson says President Donald Trump can’t direct states in matters that are up to state law.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Indigenous activist Leonard Peltier granted clemency by President Biden
item.description: The outgoing Biden administration has released Leonard Peltier from prison. Numerous activists and tribal officials have requested the release of Peltier, whom they believe to be innocent of killing two FBI agents in 1975.
item.dc:creator: Allison Herrera and Melissa Olson, MPR News

item.title: Trump sworn in as 47th president, celebrates with supporters
item.description: On a frigid holiday Monday in Washington, D.C., Donald Trump took the oath of office for a second time to become the 47th president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, PIX 11

item.title: Biden commutes the sentences of nearly 2,500 non-violent drug offenders
item.description: Biden said he is proud of his record on clemency and will “continue to review additional commutations and pardons” before he leaves office on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Court aims for fall trial date in sexual misconduct lawsuit against Fletcher
item.description: The lawsuit facing Nathan Fletcher over allegations of sexual misconduct could move to a civil trial as soon as fall 2025, as both sides continue to bump heads over text messages between the ex-supervisor's accuser and a friend.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5
